page.title: Cleft Sentences and Emphasis
page.subtitle_html: Cleft Sentences and Emphasis - C1 focus structures with it-clefts, what-clefts, and contrastive emphasis.
page.subtitle_text: C1 focus structures with it-clefts, what-clefts, and contrastive emphasis.

blocks.body: {"level":"C1","intro":"Cleft Sentences and Emphasis: C1 anchors for highlighting people, things, places, reasons, and actions with cleft patterns.","rules":[{"label":"It-cleft","text":"Use it is / was + focused element + that / who clause.","example":"It was Anna who solved the problem."},{"label":"What-cleft","text":"Use what + clause + be + focused element to emphasize an action, need, or result.","example":"What we need is a clearer plan."},{"label":"Question focus","text":"Use cleft question patterns to make the focus explicit.","example":"When was it that the company changed the policy?"}]}

blocks.heading: C1 focus
blocks.body: This compact page exists as the theory anchor for the English Sentence Builder C1 cleft sentences lesson package.
